narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection from law school stings deeply, especially when you've poured so much into your dreams of practicing law, and I want to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam as riskier than we could support—it's like piecing together a story from the chapters of your academic journey so far. At the heart of it, your Year 3 grades placed you in the bottom decile of your school, which stood out as the strongest signal of struggle, suggesting the rigors of advanced coursework might have been an uphill battle that could echo into bar prep. This was closely mirrored by your Year 1 decile, also at the bottom, painting a picture of consistent early challenges that made the model worry about building momentum over time. Your LSAT score of 37 added to that caution, as it fell short of the benchmarks that typically light the way to bar success for students like you. Even with your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.7 and upper-middle family income, which offered some stability, the model weighed those against the pattern of lower school deciles and saw them as not quite enough to offset the risks. Your background as a white male pursuing full-time studies didn't shift the scales much either, as these traits have historically aligned with similar hurdles in our data. Together, these elements told a story of potential vulnerability under the intense pressure of the bar exam, leading to our 65.8% predicted chance of not passing—a threshold we couldn't endorse for admission. But here's the empowering part: this isn't destiny; it's a snapshot based on patterns, and with targeted strategies to boost your decile performance—like focused study habits or tutoring—you could rewrite this narrative entirely. You've already shown grit with that UGPA; imagine channeling it here. We're rooting for you to prove the model wrong and chase that bar passage with everything you've got. Keep pushing forward.
